16. Imitation Learning / Behavior Cloning (BC)

Remark: We refer to robots as “embodied agent” instead of robot because you can do simulations.

Behavior cloning is just supervised learning on human recorded training set

Rollout error add noise (e.g. for a car collect data from 3 cameras and only rollout using center camera

“And then … BC methods started to get good. Really good. So good that our best manipulation system today mostly uses BC, with a sprinkle of Q learning on top to perform high-level action selection. Today, less than 20% of our research investments is on RL, and the research runway for BC-based methods feels more robust.”

The next sections show discuss ways to implement behavior cloning.

16.1. Diffusion Policy

At test time, start from random noise, iteratively denoise into a plausible action trajectory, then execute the first action or first few actions and replan.

Example: Push T task

Example: doing the dishes

16.1.1. Key challenge: evaluation

  • The metric is usually “success rate” on actual policy rollouts (i.e. you run the thing you learned and have a binary results whether the robot got it or not)

  • Doing that in simulation is fine: Very repeatable

    • But there is a big sim-to-real gap! So not generally representative
  • Doing in the real world is very, very tricky. Not easily repeatable, very difficult to reproduce results exactly.

16.2. VLAs

Use LLM to output actions and detokenize the actions. Need to fine tune with action-token labels.

Fine tuning example
  1. Collect robot demos:

image_t, instruction, expert_action_t

  1. Convert expert action to tokens:

Δx = +0.57 cm → Δy = −0.39 cm → …

  1. Make a training sequence:

input: [image tokens] “pick up the red block”

target output: …

  1. Fine-tune with next-token cross-entropy:

model predicts token distribution loss penalizes it unless it assigns high probability to the expert action token

Model output tokens:

Remarks

  • Instead of representing an action trajectory in the time basis, FAST represents in a frequency basis. Model outputs low frequencies and then refines it autoregressively with higher frequencies.
  • Unfortunately you can’t get OOD. LLM priors do not buy that much.
